text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Aisha Khalid  
St George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Atkinson Morley Neuroscience Centre, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Aisha Khalid,  

Michael Turner, DOB: 15-02-1991, Hospital No: Q472839 NHS No. 9012735648  
82 Elm Grove, Surbiton, KT6 7EW 

Diagnoses:  

Focal epilepsy with motor onset (focal clonic), likely right frontal origin  
MRI brain (March 2024): No focal structural abnormality identified  
EEG (April 2024): Frequent right frontal interictal sharp waves

Current Medication:  

Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
Clobazam 5 mg nocte (as intermittent add-on for clusters)  
Rescue: Buccal midazolam 10 mg PRN for prolonged generalised tonic-clonic seizure

Plan:  

- Continue Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
- Introduce lacosamide titration to 100 mg twice daily over 2 weeks if clusters persist despite current regimen  
- Continue Clobazam 5 mg nocte for 7 days during cluster periods only  
- Safety counselling reinforced: sleep hygiene, alcohol moderation, and driving regulations discussed  
- Provide updated seizure action plan to patient and employer  
- Follow-up in epilepsy clinic in 4 months, earlier if generalised events recur

I reviewed Michael Turner in my neurology clinic today. He describes ongoing focal events consistent with his known focal epilepsy. Specifically, focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters. Generalised tonic-clonic seizures have been reported only twice in the past five years. He reports that sleep deprivation and high work-related stress tend to precipitate clusters. There has been no loss of awareness during the focal clonic episodes, which typically involve rhythmic jerking of the left hand and forearm lasting 30–60 seconds, sometimes spreading to the upper arm. Postictally, he experiences transient fatigue but no confusion. 

He has tolerated Levetiracetam without mood disturbance, though he notes mild irritability during periods of poor sleep. Clobazam has been used intermittently during cluster weeks with partial benefit. We discussed options to improve control of focal motor seizures, including adjunctive lacosamide. He is agreeable to consider this if the current pattern persists. We reviewed seizure first-aid, rescue medication use, and avoidance of identified triggers. Driving regulations and SUDEP information were explained; he confirms he is currently driving within DVLA guidance as he has not had impaired-awareness or generalised tonic-clonic seizures for over 12 months. 

I will review him in four months. He will contact the clinic earlier if there is any increase in seizure severity, emergence of impaired awareness, or further generalised tonic-clonic seizures. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters
focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters. Generalised tonic-clonic seizures have been reported only twice in the past five years

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure frequency: The letter states, "focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters." This provides a clear numeric frequency for focal aware seizures. It also states "Generalised tonic-clonic seizures have been reported only twice in the past five years," which is a lower frequency than 5 per week. Rule for multiple frequencies: select the highest frequency across seizure types. Therefore, use 5 per week. No evidence of seizure freedom for ≥6 months. Output normalized to allowed format.
seizure_frequency_number: focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters
reference: focal clonic with retained awareness are experienced 5 times weekly, sometimes in clusters. Generalised tonic-clonic seizures have been reported only twice in the past five years
